title: centaurea micranthos subsp. kemulariae
intro: Centaurea micranthos subsp. kemulariae, also known as mountain cornflower, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the daisy family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various ailments, including wounds and infections. Centaurea micranthos subsp. kemulariae is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
